citations.description: Phrase split: 'apply the advisory findings' ends line 136; 'that genuinely improve the plan' starts line 137
Phrase split: 'apply the advisory findings that genuinely' ends line 135; 'improve the plan before claiming' on line 136

findings.criterion: AC3 positive proof: `grep -rn "apply the advisory findings that genuinely improve the plan" examples/claude-skills/` returns the softened lines in BOTH rebar-implement/SKILL.md and rebar-brainstorm/SKILL.md
findings.detail: The required phrase 'apply the advisory findings that genuinely improve the plan' does not appear on a single line in either skill file, so the specified grep returns no matches. In rebar-implement/SKILL.md:136-137 the phrase is split: line 136 ends with 'apply the advisory findings' and line 137 begins 'that genuinely improve the plan'. In rebar-brainstorm/SKILL.md:135-136 the phrase is split: line 135 ends with 'apply the advisory findings that genuinely' and line 136 begins 'improve the plan before claiming'. A standard line-by-line grep for the full string would return NOTHING from either file, failing the AC's stated two-sided proof.
findings.remediation: Reflow one of the lines in each SKILL.md so the full phrase 'apply the advisory findings that genuinely improve the plan' fits on a single line — e.g. ensure line 136 of rebar-implement reads '...apply the advisory findings that genuinely improve the plan...' without a mid-phrase line break, and similarly for rebar-brainstorm.

data.remediation: How to resolve the unmet criteria: for each one, decide whether the work is genuinely incomplete or simply undocumented. If a requirement is already satisfied but the proof is not discoverable from the code alone, add a comment to this ticket that documents the evidence — cite the concrete artifacts that meet the requirement (file paths and line ranges, commands and their output, links, or the reasoning that ties the work to the criterion). The completion verifier reads this ticket's comments, so evidence you record there is taken into account on the next verification. For any criterion whose work is genuinely unfinished, complete it, then re-verify.
